title: Homepage chainsaw autoplay improvements + Camp Riley show + generated image mapping updates

requirements.summary: Attempt to autoplay looping chainsaw background audio on initial load while keeping the site fully usable when autoplay is blocked, and ensure user-gesture controls can start playback.
acceptanceCriteria: On a fresh load with default sound preferences, the app attempts to start /assets/audio/chainsaw-loop.mp3 automatically.
If autoplay is blocked by the browser, the page still renders and navigation/scrolling works normally (no blocking modal or full-screen gate).
When the user clicks the existing "Enable sound" control, playback starts successfully (chainsaw preferred; fallback allowed if needed).
Audio loops continuously once started and respects the saved volume preference.

file_operations.description: Ensure the initial page load triggers a non-blocking autoplay attempt for the looping chainsaw audio when sound preferences are enabled, and that the gesture-based retry callback reliably re-attempts playback (chainsaw-first with fallback) while keeping volume/loop behavior consistent with saved preferences.

Verify the existing non-blocking "Enable sound" control properly triggers the gesture-based start callback when autoplay is blocked (including when re-enabling sound), without preventing normal navigation or requiring a blocking UI.

requirements.summary: Add the "Camp Riley" show (kid show, Coming 2027) to the Live Shows list with an original English description.
acceptanceCriteria: "Camp Riley" appears in the Live Shows section alongside existing shows.
The description is in English, reads as a kid-friendly spooky/fun teaser, and clearly indicates it is Coming 2027.
No TypeScript errors occur from the added show entry (e.g., required fields match existing show objects).

file_operations.description: Add a new show object for "Camp Riley" with a kid-friendly spooky/fun teaser description that clearly states it is Coming 2027, matching the existing shows array shape so it renders in the Live Shows section without TypeScript issues.

requirements.summary: Add and wire an AI-generated image for "Camp Riley" so it renders on the show card via the generatedImages mapping.
acceptanceCriteria: A new image file exists under frontend/public/assets/generated with the specified filename.
frontend/src/content/generatedImages.ts includes a shows mapping entry for "Camp Riley" that points to the new asset path.
The Camp Riley show card displays the image without runtime errors (image loads successfully).

file_operations.description: Add a generatedImages.shows mapping entry for "Camp Riley" pointing to the static asset path for frontend/public/assets/generated/show-camp-riley.dim_1400x900.jpg so ShowsSection renders the image.

requirements.summary: Replace the "Grave danger dace" scare zone image mapping with an AI-generated "Undead Dance Party" image.
acceptanceCriteria: A new image file exists under frontend/public/assets/generated with the specified filename.
frontend/src/content/generatedImages.ts is updated so the scareZones entry for "Grave danger dace" points to the new undead dance party asset path.
The Scare Zones section renders the "Grave danger dace" card using the new image (no broken image).

file_operations.description: Update generatedImages.scareZones so the "Grave danger dace" entry points to the static asset path for frontend/public/assets/generated/zone-undead-dance-party.dim_1400x900.jpg, replacing the prior image mapping used by ScareZonesSection.
